management_notes: G2 Goldfields was built by a focused team that identified and advanced the Oko West discovery in Guyana through multiple discovery announcements to an independent PEA showing a $2.5B, 14-year mine. The C$3.0B acquisition by G Mining Ventures (announced April 9, 2026) at C$10.84 per share validates management's execution. The team demonstrated exploration discipline in a jurisdictionally complex setting (Guyana) and successfully grew the resource from an initial high-grade surface discovery to 3.53 Moz of combined Indicated and Inferred resources. The negotiated acquisition price and structure — including a G3 SpinCo containing exploration upside — reflects sophisticated negotiation. Score tempered by the fact that the company did not advance to production independently.
geology_notes: Oko West is an exceptional high-grade gold system in Guyana. November 2025 NI 43-101 MRE across all five discoveries: Indicated: 15,571,000 tonnes at 3.24 g/t Au for 1,620,600 oz Au; Inferred: 17,970,000 tonnes at 3.31 g/t Au for 1,910,300 oz Au. Total combined resources: 3,530,900 oz Au at an average grade of approximately 3.28 g/t. This is an extremely high-grade resource by global standards — most large-scale gold mines operate at grades well below 1.5 g/t. The PEA filed in late 2025 outlined a $2.5B, 14-year mine. Combined with GMIN's adjacent Oko concession (which hosts the producing Oko West Mine), the strategic fit is obvious and the geology has tier-one characteristics.
capital_notes: As of year-end 2025: 240,048,848 shares outstanding. The acquisition by G Mining Ventures values each G2 share at C$10.84 (plus the value of G3 SpinCo), implying a fully diluted equity value of approximately C$3.0B. With 240M basic shares and the acquisition announced, the capital structure question is largely moot from an investor standpoint — this is now a risk-arb situation. The overhang concern is the pending close timeline (expected early Q3 2026) and the SpinCo value uncertainty.
catalyst_notes: The G Mining Ventures acquisition at C$10.84/share is the primary remaining catalyst. Expected close early Q3 2026, pending court approval and shareholder votes. Upside catalysts: G3 SpinCo value could be meaningful if remaining exploration assets are monetized; deal could attract a higher competing bid, though this appears unlikely given the strategic fit and deal structure. Downside risks: deal break (low probability), regulatory approval delays in Guyana or Canada. For new investors, most of the easy money has already been made — the stock is trading at/near the acquisition price.
acquisition_notes: The acquisition by G Mining Ventures at C$10.84/share (implying ~C$3.0B fully diluted equity value inclusive of G3 SpinCo) validates Oko West's tier-one quality. GMIN's adjacent Oko concession creates unique strategic synergies — the combined Guyana hub is expected to create one of the largest, lowest-cost gold operations in the Americas. The deal was structured at approximately 3.5-4x NAV relative to the pre-announcement trading price, consistent with premium take-out multiples for high-grade Latin American/Caribbean gold developers. Any remaining acquisition upside requires a competing bid.
analyst_summary: G2 Goldfields owns the Oko West discovery in Guyana — one of the highest-grade large-scale gold systems discovered in the Americas in the past decade, with 3.53 Moz at 3.28 g/t Au across five discoveries. The C$3.0B acquisition by G Mining Ventures, announced April 9, 2026 at C$10.84/share, validates the asset quality. For most investors the trade is now a risk-arb: buy at a small discount to the C$10.84 acquisition price, collect the spread at closing (expected Q3 2026), plus any G3 SpinCo value. The upside is limited and the risk is deal break or delay. Watch from the sidelines unless the SpinCo exploration assets offer a compelling sum-of-parts reason to hold through the close.
